name: YouTube
description: Online video-sharing and social media platform owned by Google where users can upload, view and share videos.

years.notes: First standalone YouTube play-button icon — introduced December 2013 alongside the refined 'YouTube' wordmark and used through August 2017. Horizontal rounded-rectangle 'tube' with red vertical gradient (#E52D27 → #BF171D) and white triangle. Source artwork is 1024×721; placed centred in the 24×24 viewBox via uniform transform (scaled to width, vertically centred with 3.55 unit padding top/bottom).

August 2017 rebrand by YouTube Creative Lab — flat solid red (#FF0000), updated geometry with rounded-corner 'lozenge' play-button and refined proportions. Mark replaced the 2013 gradient version on 29 August 2017 and remained primary until February 2025. Path data sourced from the YouTube embed-button artwork (also mirrored as simple-icons #FF0000 reference).

June 2024 brand refresh — same play-button geometry as 2017 with a slightly pinker red (#FF0033) and a thinner companion wordmark ('YouTube New' typeface). Promoted to primary mark on 6 February 2025.

notes: Pre-2013 wordmark millésimes (2005-2006 launch wordmark, 2006-2011 'Tube'-with-shadow wordmark, 2011-2013 flat-red wordmark) were not added. These were horizontal 'YouTube' wordmarks (≈2.5:1 aspect) where the iconic visual element is the typography itself; faithful reproduction at 24×24 would require redrawing individual letterforms by hand from rasters, which crosses into invention per .claude/agents/icon-fetcher.md. The earliest authentic standalone play-button mark (2013) is the first millésime that can be reproduced faithfully at icon scale. The icon library convention (simple-icons, font-awesome, lucide) is to use the play-button mark for all eras of YouTube.
